Necklace of Superiority  
 

Lore Item
Slot: NECK
AC: 5
STR: +5 DEX: +5 STA: +5 CHA: +5 WIS: +5 INT: +5 AGI: +5 HP: +10 MANA: +10 ENDUR: +10
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Focus: Spell Haste II
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Necklace of Superiority
Lore Item
Slot: NECK
AC: 5
STR: +5 DEX: +5 STA: +5 CHA: +5 WIS: +5 INT: +5 AGI: +5 HP: +10 MANA: +10 ENDUR: +10
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Focus: Spell Haste II
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Jewelry
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:250 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:17476
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-18 11:56:56
Page Updated:Sun Dec 13th, 2020

Expansion: Legacy of Ykesha
Average Price: 14,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Howling Stones (Charasis)
NPC Name
Drusella Sathir
The Undertaker Lord


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Howling Stones (Charasis)

Necklace of Superiority item window 2017

Uploaded September 19th, 2017 by snailish
Send a correction
Post Comment
Undertaker Lord drop
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2017 at 2:26 AM Rating: Good
Bobbybick
Guru
528 posts
From personal, and guildmate experience on TLPs, this item only begins to drop off Undertaker Lord once servers are in Luclin and beyond. TLP players looking for this item in Kunark era will have to go after Drusella.

Admittedly, it COULD drop during Kunark but from my own experiences sitting in Howling Stones and from others that never saw him drop it during that period it would have to be extremely rare. Currently in LDoN era on Phinigel I've seen the Undertaker Lord drop this item 2 out of 5 kills which doesn't match my experiences in Kunark whatsoever.

Necklace of power
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2004 at 3:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755278500
View User Forum
17 posts
Just thinking here. I just did the key quest and remebered that the frog that gives the key asks about a neclace of power after he hands you the key. Well this comes from the zone he gives the key to and his master was a necro just like drusella. I will prolly have to kill her a few times to get my urn from the sound of it. If i get one of these im gonna hand it to the frog and see what he says about it. wish me luck i dont think npcs eat items anymore but i would hate to waste a good item like this. But if i get it once i can get it again.

Haste II and items with effects
QuoteReply
# Jan 18 2003 at 3:00 PM Rating: Decent
Addikeys
Scholar
539 posts
Would this focus effect (Haste II) help to increase the casting speed of items with effects? For example, the Deepwater helmet takes about 10 seconds to cast. Would wearing the Necklace of Superiority help to decrease that casting time? I'd appreciate any information regarding this. Thank you.
____________________________
"Citing your sources isn't spoon feeding, it's basic 101 if you're making an argument."-Jophiel
▲ Page top
RE: Haste II and items with effects
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2003 at 10:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248482867
71 posts
Nothing changes the time an item takes to cast an effect.
▲ Page top
RE: Haste II and items with effects
QuoteReply
# Mar 27 2003 at 12:11 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966302790
15 posts
Incorrect. Cast times for clicky gear are reduced by mage-summoned focus rings and cleric's Blessing of Faith/Reverence line of spells. Both reduce my ES vambs casting time from 10 sec to 8 sec. As far as permanent effects, I have no idea.
▲ Page top
RE: Haste II and items with effects
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2003 at 4:00 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788149302
16 posts
spell haste 2 wouldnt help Kunark armor cast the cap for spell haste 2 is 44 and armor isnt usable til 45+ if i remeber right. Sooo in that sense I dont think it would but if you have spell haste 3 or 4 it should increase the casting speed.
▲ Page top
Necklace
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2002 at 7:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771948127
Scholar
46 posts
Was previously using a Clattering Bone Necklace for my SK, which seems to be the preferred choice amongst the majority for that quality of item.

But when I ran into this little gem, I bought it, instantly. Clattering is 8ac 6str 6sta 30hp. I loose 3 raw AC, 1str, 1sta and 20hp. But I gain 5agi, 5dex, 5int, 10 mana, 5 to all my saves, as well as a focus effect that speeds up all my spells (44 and below).

For certain classes, like us hyrbids, rangers pallies and SKs, these things are mustard, it's got everything we need. I doubt I'll replace this any time soon, to be quite honest. They've come down in price, mabye I got lucky, but I got this one for 5k cash.

Awesome neckpiece, mostly for hybrids but great for all.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 03 2002 at 2:27 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) As much as I'd like to agree with you on the hybrid part...this necklace would kick some serious *** for all classes...monks/warriors/rogues would all benefit from the +5 to everystat rangers/sks/pallys would too along with spell haste & casters gotta enjoy the same benefits. Relatively speaking, I don't think anyone who would look at this piece would decide to give it up, unless of course they had better...but usually not true.
RE: Necklace
QuoteReply
# May 16 2003 at 2:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758666437
View User Forum
7 posts
Sadly, this necklace would be crap for a necro - black sapphire neck (65hp/mana) is much better simply for the HP. my 2cp

▲ Page top
Now with focus effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2002 at 9:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965890681
14 posts
As of the June 5 patch, this item now has the focus effect: extend affliction I.

This means that all your level 20 and lower DoT's and debuffs will last 20% longer.

I can only assume that VI considers this to be a twink item, which flys in the face of the average level of 50 of the 169 characters registered here as wearing this.

Perhaps they used their RNG to assign affects to items?
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 07 2002 at 8:38 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) "Perhaps they used their RNG to assign affects to items?"
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 16 2002 at 2:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) perhaps you're an idiot. I thouhgt it was quite funny.

question for you
QuoteReply
# May 16 2002 at 1:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249641124
79 posts
i have a question:i'm thinking this would be easier to get then the NToV talismen's. but i could be wrong.
▲ Page top
RE: question for you
QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2002 at 9:36 PM Rating: Default
Brakiss
View User Forum
Scholar
100 posts
This is much easier to obtain if you get her to drop the darn thing. Took her out with a group of 5 people: 60 war, rog, mnk, dru, 56 cle at 20m. The only downer about this particular camp is you MUST have a FD puller or the aggro from all the HT's will wipe you out regardless of the chanter due to some abysmal pathing at that camp. Tried again last night with 60 war, rog, dru, 58 enc, 56 cle, and 55 rog and w/o the FD puller the hallway we were in looked like a raid was happening due to all the HT aggro.
NToV requires a few highly skilled groups to just traverse through there. Hope this helps and good luck wishing for a necklace to drop )




Edited, Tue Oct 15 05:56:47 2002
____________________________
Brakiss Dooku (retired)
Archon of Vazaelle
<Walkers>
Adalric Brandl <Walkers>
Deathspeaker of Innoruuk


▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 18 2002 at 12:34 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)
RE: question for you
QuoteReply
# Sep 18 2002 at 12:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253459527
45 posts
Disagree this can and has been done by myself and friends without an FD puller. Can have my 60 war try his best to not pull the whole group, OR have a pally pounce in with DA rolling and pop it up soon as agro comes. If he is only person on hate list the agro will stick to him despite that DA throws him to bottom of hate list while activated. Chanter can mez one by one at will as needed with fast casting mezzes until gains agro, pally breaks DA and stuns/taunts off as the chanter uses AE stuns to drop others off of him long enough to toss another mez, etc. Need a chanter and pally who know how to work the game, but in no way is this not doable without an FD puller.
Spunkmidg/Supmidg/Weemidg
▲ Page top
RE: question for you
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2002 at 5:14 AM Rating: Excellent

#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 18 2001 at 8:29 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) are you level 1 with your guild tunic moron ?
#Anonymous, Posted: May 30 2001 at 4:32 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Awesome, thanks for the enlightenment, many eq players across the globe will say you now correctly thanks to your valiant efforts. #Anonymous, Posted: Dec 10 2000 at 7:32 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I have seen better
RE: wussness
QuoteReply
# Jan 24 2001 at 1:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751366806
18 posts
this is a very nice necklace indeed, especially if you play on a PvP server. Right now I wear a white gold necklace (5cha 7svmagic 7svcold 7svfire) but I would gladly lose the 2 per resist to gain the extra stats. *sigh* it is so hard to keep good melee stats while keeping good resists as well.
▲ Page top
RE: wussness
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2000 at 11:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750254454
21 posts
seen better WHAT? Other then an epic weapon, I would say no item in this game comes close (found so far, anyway) to this necklace. 5 to ALL stats, 10 to mana/HP

There may be a couple (rare) items that have better total stats in YOUR class requirements (ac/int/cha/hp/mana, for enchanter, as an example) but if you only focus on a couple stats, you're not optimizing your character.

Dex/Sta/Agi/str -- all help combat, both offensively and defensivly -- good for all classes
ac - one of the better (not greatest) AC neck pieces in game -- good for all classes
HP - shrug...80hp = 1 hit in RoK/SoV
mana - shrug...10mana = not much
resists -- if you don't know the value of resists, I hope you're a blue server necro, or else you don't know the game that well

add it all up and what's it =? BEST neckpiece in the game for all classes, although there may be a couple pieces that are "better" in a few (very few) stats.
▲ Page top
RE: wussness
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2001 at 9:09 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246460789
View User Forum
513 posts
Well, I do have to agree that it's pretty great... but now all the named Dragons in Velious have their own talismans which are equal to this, if not better.

What I want to know is this... is there possibly a quest from the Storm Giant King (Or maybe Rallos Zek himself) that involves gathering all of those talismans? Wouldn't that be something to see!
▲ Page top
RE: wussness
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2001 at 6:47 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Funny you should mention it... there is!!

The quest is given in Dragon Necropolis. You need to collect all five talismans to receive the key to the Sleepers Tomb. There is more than just this to this quest... Look it up right here at this site. VERY tough quest... but the loot there in Sleepers must be WELL worth the work.
It had better be anyways...

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 13 2001 at 10:35 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) You idiot you only need to collect 1 tailsman to get the key to sleepers. But there are 5! dragons that drop the type of tailsman you need. Its just this site is to stupid to realize this and puts up you need 5 shessh.
